name: Alarm Monitor
description: Monitors a tag against high/low limits and generates alarm status
author: NISystem

published_tags.name: py.${config.input_tag}_InAlarm
published_tags.description: 1 if any alarm active, 0 if OK

code:
# Alarm Monitor with Hysteresis
# Alarm States: 0=OK, 1=Low, 2=LowLow, 3=High, 4=HighHigh

input_tag = config.get('input_tag', 'Temperature_1')
hh = config.get('high_high_limit', 100)
h = config.get('high_limit', 80)
l = config.get('low_limit', 20)
ll = config.get('low_low_limit', 10)
db = config.get('deadband', 1.0)

value = tags.get(input_tag, 0)

# Get previous state for hysteresis
prev_state = state.get('alarm_state', 0)

# Determine new alarm state with deadband
if value >= hh:
    alarm_state = 4  # HighHigh
elif value >= h and (prev_state >= 3 or value >= h + db):
    alarm_state = 3  # High
elif value <= ll:
    alarm_state = 2  # LowLow
elif value <= l and (prev_state in [1, 2] or value <= l - db):
    alarm_state = 1  # Low
else:
    # Apply deadband for return to normal
    if prev_state == 3 and value > h - db:
        alarm_state = 3
    elif prev_state == 1 and value < l + db:
        alarm_state = 1
    else:
        alarm_state = 0  # OK

state['alarm_state'] = alarm_state

publish(f'py.{input_tag}_AlarmState', alarm_state)
publish(f'py.{input_tag}_InAlarm', 1 if alarm_state > 0 else 0)
